items.headline: U.S. Markets Exhibit Mixed Performance Amid Economic Data Anticipation and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: U.S. stock futures indicated a mixed opening on Monday, July 13, as markets braced for upcoming inflation data and the commencement of Q2 earnings season. Geopolitical tensions, particularly renewed friction in the Middle East, continue to influence investor sentiment, leading to elevated oil prices, although some gains were later tempered. The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds rate target at 3.5-3.75%, reinforcing a steady monetary policy stance amidst solid economic expansion and strong productivity growth. AI-related fears also weighed on some tech stocks.

items.headline: Twin Vee Powercats Co Surges on Merger News
items.summary: Twin Vee Powercats Co ($VEEE) shares skyrocketed 140% to $11.57 after the company announced a merger agreement to combine its boating operations with a USFM subsidiary in Greenland.

items.headline: Q32 Bio Inc Jumps on Positive Clinical Trial Results
items.summary: Shares of Q32 Bio Inc ($QTTB) surged 67% to $18.76 after the company announced positive 36-week topline results from Part B of its SIGNAL-AA Phase 2a clinical trial.

items.headline: Agenus Inc Gains on Funding for Cancer Drug Trial
items.summary: Agenus Inc ($AGEN) shares were up 62% to $5.42 following the announcement of raising $85 million to fund its colon cancer drug trial.

items.headline: WD-40 Company Reports Strong Q3 Earnings
items.summary: Shares of WD-40 Company ($WDFC) surged 10.7% after the company reported third-quarter 2026 earnings of $2.33 per share, significantly beating analyst estimates.

items.headline: BNB Plus Corp Plummets on Delisting Notification
items.summary: BNB Plus Corp ($BNBX) shares dropped 52% to $0.18 after the company announced it received a Nasdaq delisting notification.

items.headline: YSX Tech Co Ltd Falls on Earnings Decline
items.summary: Shares of YSX Tech Co Ltd ($YSXT) were down 39% to $0.62 after the company reported a year-over-year decrease in FY26 EPS results.

items.headline: Jiuzi Holdings Inc Experiences Significant Decline
items.summary: Jiuzi Holdings Inc ($JZXN) was down 35% to $1.40 today, despite a jump on Friday related to AI intelligent imaging news.

items.headline: SK Hynix ADRs Decline in Premarket Trading
items.summary: SK Hynix ($SKHY) ADR shares were trading nearly 8% lower ahead of the Wall Street opening bell, on track to give back much of the gains from its strong Nasdaq debut on Friday.

items.headline: Energy and Healthcare Lead, Tech and Materials Lag in Sector Rotation
items.summary: Energy shares jumped 2.1% on Monday, marking it as a leading sector alongside Healthcare, which has reclaimed a leadership position. Conversely, information technology stocks fell by 1.1%, while materials lagged, down 2.15% for the week. Sector rotation analysis indicates that Financials remain a primary overweight, and Consumer Discretionary is showing improvement.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases Anticipated This Week, Including June CPI
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting a busy week of economic data, with particular focus on the U.S. June CPI report, which is expected to slow below 4%. Other key releases include core CPI, the Michigan consumer sentiment index, retail sales, and housing data. The Federal Reserve's Beige Book on economic conditions will also be released.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Commences with Major Bank Reports
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season has officially kicked off, with several major U.S. banks such as JPMorgan Chase ($JPM), Citigroup ($C), Bank of America ($BAC), Goldman Sachs ($GS), Wells Fargo ($WFC), and Morgan Stanley ($MS) scheduled to report this week. Netflix ($NFLX), Johnson & Johnson ($JNJ), and TSMC ($TSM) are also among the anticipated reporters. WD-40 Company ($WDFC) delivered strong Q3 earnings, while Delta Air Lines ($DAL) missed revenue estimates.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Reinforces Steady Monetary Policy Stance
items.summary: The Federal Reserve reaffirmed its commitment to a steady monetary policy, maintaining its federal funds rate target at 3.5-3.75%. The FOMC minutes from the June meeting and the July Monetary Policy Report underscored policy continuity, citing solid economic expansion, strong productivity growth, and balanced labor market conditions. Federal Reserve Chair Walsh is scheduled to testify before Congress on the semiannual monetary policy report.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Continue to Influence Market Volatility
items.summary: Renewed friction between the U.S. and Iran, particularly escalations in the Middle East, continue to be a significant factor for market participants, impacting oil prices and contributing to investor uncertainty. Despite initial surges in oil prices, some gains were later given back as diplomacy efforts emerged.
